title: polypogon tenellus var. drummondii
intro: Polypogon tenellus var. drummondii, also known as Drummond's Hairgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion. Herbal preparations of Polypogon tenellus var. drummondii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
